summary: Small unmerged lbcardnew matching improvement for lb_8001B14C. The PR removed a local alias for lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries and wrote snapshot-entry fields directly through the global struct, which the author reported matched the target code shape more closely. It improved main/melee/lb/lbcardnew to 40/41 functions at 100%, with lb_8001B14C still at 98.77124% due to an r5/r6 allocation swap in a compiler-unrolled linked-list copy loop.
what_changed: In lb_8001B14C, removed the local variable lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry* snapshot_entries.
Changed writes in the linked-list copy loop from snapshot_entries[j].time/file_no/blocks to direct lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries[j].time/file_no/blocks accesses.
Changed the cleanup/default fill loop from snapshot_entries[i].file_no = -1 to lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries[i].file_no = -1.
No behavior change is evident from the diff; this is a code-shape adjustment for matching.
smart_moves: Avoided a convenient local alias when the target apparently retained direct global-field addressing; this can influence register allocation and emitted load/store shape.
Kept the edit tightly scoped to the snapshot-entry writes inside lb_8001B14C rather than reshaping the surrounding card/stat/node logic.
Documented the exact remaining blocker: an r5/r6 allocation swap in the compiler-unrolled linked-list copy loop.
Included verification with configure.py, ninja, and the function-match status for main/melee/lb/lbcardnew.
decomp_lessons: For matching, replacing a global struct member with a local pointer alias is not always neutral; even equivalent C can alter register allocation and address calculation.
When the target code appears to write through a global struct field repeatedly, direct accesses such as lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries[index] may match better than caching the member pointer.
Small decomp improvements should record the remaining diff precisely, especially when only a register allocation swap remains.
A function can remain just under full match even after source-level control/data shape improvements if the compiler allocates equivalent values to different registers in an unrolled loop.
naming_conventions: Global library state uses the generated-style symbol lb_80432A68.
Snapshot-list entry type is named lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry.
Linked-list helper nodes are referred to as struct SnapshotNode.
The PR title follows subsystem prefix style: "lb: improve lbcardnew snapshot listing".
Field names in the snapshot entry include time, file_no, and blocks.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use direct global member access to influence generated code shape instead of introducing a local pointer alias.
Preserve the apparent target access pattern across both populated entries and default/invalid entries.
Track residual mismatches in terms of register allocation, here the reported r5/r6 swap in a compiler-unrolled linked-list copy loop.
Do not assume pointer hoisting improves matching; sometimes repeated global-member expressions are closer to the original compiler output.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was closed without merge according to the context; no explicit reason for closure is included in the provided data.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching lb_8001B14C to see whether the r5/r6 allocation swap was resolved.
Search for other cases where direct lb_80432A68 field access matched better than a local alias.
Check whether this closed-unmerged patch was superseded by another lbcardnew matching PR.
Compare generated assembly before and after removing local aliases for global struct members in lb functions.
